check: Content demonstrates first-hand experience where relevant (reviews, tutorials, comparisons)
description: For content types where firsthand experience matters (product reviews, how-to guides, comparisons), the content shows evidence of actual use/testing rather than purely synthesized/researched information
why_it_matters: This is the 'Experience' addition to E-A-T that Google's guidelines specifically call out — content demonstrating genuine hands-on experience is treated as a distinct, valuable signal from purely well-researched-but-secondhand content

check: Original photos/media used for experience-based content, not stock imagery
description: Reviews and hands-on content include original photos/video of the actual product/process rather than manufacturer stock images
why_it_matters: Original media is a strong, hard-to-fake experience signal — both for search engines and for reader trust, since stock imagery on a 'review' visibly undermines its credibility

check: Author bylines identify a real person with relevant credentials
description: Content, especially YMYL (Your Money or Your Life) topics like health/finance/legal, is attributed to a named author with a bio establishing relevant expertise
why_it_matters: Anonymous or generic 'Admin' bylines are a weak trust signal, particularly for topics where incorrect information could cause real harm — this is one of the most consistently emphasized E-E-A-T factors for YMYL content

check: Author bio pages exist and are linked from every article
description: Each author has a dedicated bio page (credentials, other published work, relevant background) linked from their byline on every piece they've written
why_it_matters: A byline name with no accessible bio page provides no way for a reader (or a search engine evaluating expertise signals) to verify the claimed expertise

check: Site/author is cited or linked by other reputable sources in the topic area
description: The site or its authors are referenced, linked, or cited by other credible sources within the same topic domain, not existing in isolation
why_it_matters: External validation from other authoritative sources in the same field is a core authoritativeness signal that's difficult to fabricate and correspondingly valuable when genuinely earned

check: About/company page clearly establishes who is behind the site and why they're credible on this topic
description: An About page (or equivalent) explains the organization/individual's background, credentials, and connection to the topics covered
why_it_matters: This is a foundational trust signal that both users and search engines' quality raters explicitly evaluate — a site with no discoverable answer to 'who made this and why should I trust them' underperforms on trust-dependent topics

check: Contact information and business details are genuine and verifiable
description: A real contact method (address, phone, email — appropriate to the business type) is present and accurate, not obscured or missing
why_it_matters: This overlaps directly with local SEO/NAP consistency and is a basic trust signal — its absence is disproportionately noticed on YMYL and e-commerce sites specifically

check: Claims backed by citations/sources where appropriate, especially statistics and factual claims
description: Statistical claims, factual assertions, and non-obvious claims link to or cite their original source rather than being stated as unsupported assertions
why_it_matters: Unsupported factual claims are both a trust liability and increasingly scrutinized as AI-generated content (which can hallucinate confident-sounding but unsupported claims) has become more common

check: Reviews/testimonials are genuine, not fabricated or incentivized without disclosure
description: Displayed reviews and testimonials are real, and any incentivized reviews (free product, payment) are disclosed per applicable regulations (e.g. FTC guidelines)
why_it_matters: Fabricated reviews are both an ethical and regulatory risk (FTC enforcement) and, when discovered, cause disproportionate trust damage relative to the ranking benefit they were meant to provide

check: Content covers the full scope of what a knowledgeable searcher would expect, not a narrow slice
description: Comprehensive content addresses the natural sub-questions and related considerations a topic raises, rather than answering only the literal query and stopping
why_it_matters: Depth relative to genuine user need (not artificial word-count padding) is a strong quality signal — the goal is completeness of value, not length for its own sake

check: Content is reviewed and updated periodically, especially for time-sensitive topics
description: Content on topics that change over time (pricing, regulations, best practices, product details) is reviewed on a defined cadence and updated with genuine changes, not just a bumped date
why_it_matters: Stale content on a changing topic is both a user-trust liability (outdated information) and a ranking liability as search engines favor demonstrably current information for time-sensitive queries

check: Outdated/incorrect content is updated or removed, not left indefinitely
description: Content later found to be inaccurate or obsolete is corrected or removed/redirected rather than left live and unaddressed
why_it_matters: Accumulating outdated content across a large site both actively misleads users and, in aggregate, can affect a site's overall perceived quality and trustworthiness

check: AI-assisted content is edited for accuracy, originality, and genuine value, not published unedited
description: Content produced with AI assistance goes through human review confirming factual accuracy, adding genuine perspective/experience, and removing generic filler before publishing
why_it_matters: Unedited, generic AI output is both a quality-signal risk and increasingly detectable as a pattern — the fix is not avoiding AI tools, it's ensuring the published result has genuine editorial value added

check: Content avoids generic AI-writing tells (repetitive structure, hedge-everything phrasing, no specific examples)
description: Published content reads as specific and grounded — concrete examples, specific numbers/details, a clear point of view — rather than generically hedged, structurally repetitive text
why_it_matters: Beyond any algorithmic detection question, this pattern is a genuine, independently-valid quality problem: generic, non-committal writing is less useful to readers regardless of how it was produced

check: Content structured for passage-level extraction (self-contained paragraphs that answer a question completely)
description: Key paragraphs are written to stand alone as a complete answer if extracted in isolation, since AI Overviews/chat answer engines typically quote a passage, not a full page
why_it_matters: This directly supports citation likelihood in AI Overviews, ChatGPT search, and Perplexity — these systems extract and cite specific passages, not entire pages, so passage-level completeness matters distinctly from overall page quality

check: Brand/entity name and key facts appear early and unambiguously in relevant content
description: The subject's name, key identifying facts, and core claims are stated clearly and early rather than requiring inference from context
why_it_matters: AI systems building an answer from a passage benefit from unambiguous entity identification early in the extracted text — content that only becomes clear several paragraphs in is more likely to be paraphrased incorrectly or skipped

check: Brand is mentioned and described consistently across the sites AI systems draw from
description: The brand's description, positioning, and key facts are consistent across its own site, review sites, and any other sources AI answer engines might synthesize from
why_it_matters: Inconsistent brand descriptions across sources make it harder for an AI system to synthesize an accurate, confident answer about the brand — consistency reduces the chance of an AI response getting brand facts wrong

check: Content addresses follow-up questions a searcher would naturally have next
description: Beyond answering the primary query, content anticipates and addresses the next question a satisfied reader would likely have
why_it_matters: This both improves genuine user satisfaction (fewer follow-up searches needed) and increases the content's value as a source for AI systems synthesizing multi-part answers

check: Content is monitored for unauthorized scraping/republishing
description: Original content is periodically checked (via a plagiarism tool or manual spot-check) for unauthorized republishing elsewhere, with DMCA action taken where warranted
why_it_matters: Scraped content republished elsewhere can occasionally outrank the original, particularly if the scraping site has stronger overall domain authority — periodic monitoring catches this before it compounds
